name: White Beans with Roasted Garlic Dressing
recipeIngredient: 15 ounces beans, Great Northern, canned; drained and rinsed
1 small red bell pepper, seeded and finely diced
2 tablespoons chives, snipped OR 2 tablespoons scallion greens, chopped
1 tablespoon oregano, fresh, chopped OR ½ teaspoon oregano, dried
3 cups chicory or other bitter lettuce, washed and torn
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Remove 1 clove of garlic from the head; peel and cut in half.
3. Rub the garlic on both sides of bread slices; cut the bread into ½-inch cubes.
4. Place the bread cubes on a baking sheet.
5. Remove the loose, papery skin from the garlic head and slice off the top (½ inch).
6. Wrap the remaining garlic in aluminum foil and place it on the baking sheet.
7. Bake for 10 minutes or until croutons are crisp and golden. Remove the croutons and set aside.
8. Continue roasting the garlic for 20 to 25 minutes longer; it should be quite soft.
9. Unwrap and let cool for 5 minutes.
10. Separate the garlic cloves and squeeze out the pulp into a small bowl.
11. Mash with a fork, then whisk in lemon juice, vegetable stock, olive oil, mustard, and cayenne.
12. In a medium-sized bowl, combine beans, red peppers, chives or scallion greens, oregano, and the reserved croutons.
13. Pour the garlic dressing over and toss until well coated.
14. Season with salt and pepper.
15. Serve immediately on a bed of chicory. Serves 2.
per serving: 546 cal; 29 g protein; 11 g fat; 88 g carb; 399 mg sodium; 0 mg chol
